title: Predicting Student Performance and Enhancing Learning Outcomes: A Data-Driven Approach Using Educational Data Mining Techniques
ml_method: Educational Data Mining (EDM): RF, LR, DT, Neural, kNN on Moodle LMS data
sample: 450 students (higher education, Moodle platform)
key_finding: EDM on Moodle data achieves 88% accuracy for student performance prediction (450 students); RF outperforms; LMS behavioral features (logins, submissions, video views) are predictive

title: Influence of artificial intelligence on higher education reform and talent cultivation in the digital intelligence era
ml_method: Random Forest (adaptive boosting) + Graph Neural Network (GNN)
sample: 300 survey participants + 4127 students + 8000 students (public platform data)
key_finding: SEOM (RF+GNN) achieves 85-97% accuracy for learning path recommendation; tested in 3 scenarios (online mixed, personalized, project-based); strong generalization ability

title: Adaptive intelligent tutoring systems for STEM education: analysis of the learning impact and effectiveness of personalized feedback
ml_method: Deep learning + NLP for personalized adaptive feedback
sample: 450 students, STEM disciplines (math, physics, programming)
key_finding: AI-ITS with DL+NLP achieves 70-85% concept mastery in STEM (450 students); high-interaction learners reach 90%+ mastery in programming; 80% rated adaptive feedback as helpful

title: Enhancing Student Academic Success Prediction Through Ensemble Learning and Image-Based Behavioral Data Transformation
ml_method: Ensemble CNN+FCN (EnCF) on image-encoded behavioral data (GAF, RP, SWT, PR)
sample: 326 students (behavioral log data)
key_finding: Novel image-encoding of 1D behavioral data + CNN+FCN ensemble achieves 98% accuracy (326 students); Gramian Angular Field encoding outperforms others; addresses class imbalance

title: Personalized Learning Through MBTI Prediction: A Deep Learning Approach Integrated With Learner Profile Ontology
ml_method: Deep Learning + MBTI personality prediction + Learner Profile Ontology
sample: Not accessible from IEEE preview
key_finding: MBTI personality prediction + DL + ontology for personalized e-learning; adapts content to individual learning styles; abstracts only accessible, full text behind IEEE paywall

title: Deep knowledge tracing and cognitive load estimation for personalized learning path generation using neural network architecture
ml_method: DKT + bidirectional Transformer + graph attention + cognitive load multimodal estimation
sample: 2,183 students, multiple educational domains
key_finding: Novel DKT+cognitive load dual-stream framework achieves 24.6% path quality improvement (2,183 students); balances knowledge acquisition with cognitive load management; multiple educational domains

title: Role of artificial intelligence in enhancing competency assessment and transforming curriculum in higher vocational education
ml_method: Regression analysis: AI-driven training + student engagement → competency scores
sample: Sample size not clearly stated; FAIR data principles applied
key_finding: AI-driven training (β=0.45) and student engagement (β=0.32) significantly predict competency (R2=0.68, p<0.001) in vocational education; demographic factors also significant

title: Empowering Education: Leveraging Clustering and Recommendations for Enhanced Student Insights
ml_method: K-means clustering + personalized recommendation system for student groups
sample: 480 students (behavioral engagement metrics)
key_finding: K-means identifies distinct student groups by engagement; personalized recommendations improve outcomes; 480 students; actionable insights for instructors

title: From Queries to Courses: SKYRAG Revolution in Learning Path Generation via Keyword-Based Document Retrieval
ml_method: LLM + RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) for learning path generation (SKYRAG)
sample: 13,373 courses; 79 students for evaluation
key_finding: SKYRAG (LLM+RAG) reduces hallucination in course recommendations; 13,373 course dataset; 79-student evaluation; keyword retrieval augmentation improves path relevance

title: A hybrid model integrating recurrent neural networks and the semi-supervised support vector machine for identification of early student dropout risk
ml_method: RNN + semi-supervised SVM hybrid for early dropout detection
sample: 18,000 students (primary dataset); multiple sub-datasets 243-4,425 records
key_finding: RNN+semi-supervised SVM achieves 91% accuracy for early dropout detection (18,000 students); semi-supervised approach handles limited labeled data; outperforms single-model baselines

title: Deep learning based knowledge tracing in intelligent tutoring systems
ml_method: DKT + dynamic graph attention network for ITS knowledge tracking
sample: Small demonstration (19-21 students); standard KT benchmark datasets likely used
key_finding: Novel DKT + dynamic graph attention for ITS; captures student-knowledge relationships dynamically; applies to COVID-era online education contexts; personalized feedback mechanism

title: Detecting Student Engagement in an Online Learning Environment Using a Machine Learning Algorithm
ml_method: ML for multi-dimensional student engagement detection (behavioral, emotional, cognitive)
sample: 325 students (local) + 32,593 (benchmark dataset)
key_finding: ML achieves 98.49% accuracy for online engagement detection (325 local + 32,593 benchmark students); multi-dimensional engagement operationalization; supports adaptive interventions

title: TSA-GRU: A Novel Hybrid Deep Learning Module for Learner Behavior Analytics in MOOCs
ml_method: Temporal Sparse Attention-Gated Recurrent Unit (TSA-GRU) for MOOC behavior analytics
sample: 3,000 students; 300,000 interaction records
key_finding: TSA-GRU captures temporal sparsity in MOOC learner behavior (3K students, 300K records); outperforms GRU/LSTM/Transformer baselines; handles irregular time-series interaction patterns

title: Evaluation of the impact of AI-driven personalized learning platform on medical students learning performance
ml_method: AI-driven personalized learning platform (pre/post quasi-experimental)
sample: n=40 medical students (n=20 experimental, n=20 control)
key_finding: AI-driven personalized platform significantly improves medical student performance (p<0.05, Cohen d); n=40, quasi-experimental; effect sizes demonstrate practical significance beyond statistical

title: Adaptive deep reinforcement learning for personalized learning path optimization in e-learning platforms
ml_method: Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) for adaptive personalized learning path optimization
sample: Not accessible (ScienceDirect CAPTCHA)
key_finding: DRL for adaptive learning path optimization in e-learning; dynamic adjustment to student state; CAEAI 2025 (top venue); ScienceDirect blocked by CAPTCHA — assessed from title/abstract only

title: Difficulty aware programming knowledge tracing via large language models
ml_method: LLM-based knowledge tracing with difficulty-awareness for programming education
sample: 100 programming problems (training); 75 problems (evaluation); standard KT benchmarks
key_finding: LLM with difficulty-aware KT achieves 94.6% AUC for programming knowledge tracing; addresses difficulty impact on knowledge state modeling; PLCodeBERT integration; directly relevant to CS programming education

title: A knowledge tracing approach with dual graph convolutional networks and positive/negative feature enhancement network
ml_method: Dual GCN + positive/negative feature enhancement network for KT
sample: 28,834 students (primary); 1,709 students (secondary dataset); multiple educational datasets
key_finding: Dual GCN + pos/neg feature enhancement outperforms traditional and DL-based KT baselines (28,834 students); addresses data sparsity and complex skill correlations; supports personalized teaching

title: A Second-Classroom Personalized Learning Path Recommendation System Based on Large Language Model Technology
ml_method: GPT-4 (LLM) for personalized learning path recommendation (extracurricular activities)
sample: 1,200 student records; 100 students evaluated; 50 students comparison set
key_finding: GPT-4 achieves 92-94% accuracy for second-classroom personalized path recommendation (1,200 records); leverages long-sequence understanding and multimodal behavior data; outperforms CF baselines

title: Research on Adaptive Education Path Dynamic Programming Algorithm Based on Reinforcement Learning and Cognitive Graphs
ml_method: Reinforcement Learning + Cognitive Graphs for dynamic adaptive learning path generation
sample: Not accessible from IEEE abstract only
key_finding: RL + cognitive graphs for dynamic adaptive path programming; addresses static model limitations; cognitive style-aware path adaptation; IEEE Access 2025

title: Student Performance Prediction Using Machine Learning Algorithms
journal: Applied Computational Intelligence and Soft Computing (Wiley)
ml_method: SVM, Decision Tree, Naive Bayes, KNN for student performance prediction
sample: 32,582 students (large dataset)
key_finding: ML comparison for student performance prediction (32,582 students); best model achieves 96% accuracy; SVM and ensemble methods outperform; provides robust benchmark

title: Enhancing academic performance prediction with temporal graph networks for massive open online courses
ml_method: Temporal Graph Networks (TGN) for MOOC academic performance prediction
sample: 3,897 students (MOOC platform)
key_finding: TGN achieves 83.22% accuracy for MOOC performance prediction (3,897 students); temporal dynamics outperform static GNN and LSTM baselines; captures time-evolving student interactions

title: Investigating the use of chatGPT as a tool for enhancing critical thinking and argumentation skills in international relations debates among undergraduate students
ml_method: ChatGPT/LLM as pedagogical tool
sample: 95 undergraduates, international relations, developing nation, RCT (n=48 exp, n=47 control)
key_finding: ChatGPT significantly improved critical thinking and argumentation vs traditional debate methods (RCT, n=95); highlights challenges/limitations in developing nation context

title: AI-Powered VR for Enhanced Learning Compared to Traditional Methods
ml_method: NLP (Meta Voice SDK, wit.ai), VR personalization, gamification
sample: 48 engineering students (24 VR+AI, 24 traditional 2D booklet), EEG headsets
key_finding: VR+AI personalization+gamification significantly improves EEG-measured engagement and learning outcomes vs traditional methods; novelty effect is a limitation; Python programming context, engineering students

title: Personality-Aware Course Recommender System Using Deep Learning for Technical and Vocational Education and Training
ml_method: Deep Learning + Big Five personality traits for course recommendation (TVET-CRS)
sample: 1356 trainees (nationwide TVET program, Pakistan)
key_finding: Personality-aware DL recommender achieves 83.95% accuracy for TVET course recommendation; Big Five traits improve personalization; addresses dropout reduction; nationwide Pakistan data

title: Evaluating and Enhancing Artificial Intelligence Models for Predicting Student Learning Outcomes
ml_method: 10 ML/DL models compared: RF, DT, SVM, KNN, LR, XGBoost, CNN, LSTM, etc.
sample: 480 students (395 training instances)
key_finding: Comprehensive comparison of 10 ML/DL models for student performance prediction (480 students); XGBoost outperforms deep learning models; provides benchmark for educational prediction

title: Prediction of Course Grades in Computer Science Higher Education Program via a Combination of Loss Functions in LSTM Model
ml_method: LSTM with combined custom loss functions for CS grade prediction
sample: 11,895 instances from CS higher education program
key_finding: LSTM with combined loss functions for CS course grade prediction (11,895 instances); custom loss functions improve prediction over standard LSTM; addresses early dropout identification

title: Prediction of Students Grade by Combining Educational Knowledge Graph and Collaborative Filtering
ml_method: Educational Knowledge Graph + Collaborative Filtering for grade prediction
sample: Not accessible from IEEE abstract
key_finding: KG+CF overcomes content-correlation blind spot of traditional collaborative filtering for grade prediction; captures course semantic relationships via knowledge graph

title: A Unified Interpretable Intelligent Learning Diagnosis Framework for Learning Performance Prediction in Intelligent Tutoring Systems
ml_method: Unified framework: psychometric models + deep learning (DINA + neural network)
sample: 4209 online learners + 2000 learners (public) + 93 offline (CL21 self-built dataset)
key_finding: Interpretable learning diagnosis framework (psychometric+DL) outperforms 11 SOTA on AUC (+5%) and RMSE (-7%); open-sources CL21 offline dataset; balances accuracy and interpretability for ITS

title: Developing a Personalized E-Learning and MOOC Recommender System in IoT-Enabled Smart Education
ml_method: IoT + Deep Learning + Collaborative Filtering for personalized MOOC recommendations
sample: 130,000+ courses dataset; 3522 course evaluation set
key_finding: IoT+DL hybrid recommender for MOOC personalization; improves NDCG vs collaborative filtering baseline; captures dynamic learner preference changes; large-scale course dataset

title: Utilizing grid search cross-validation with adaptive boosting for augmenting performance of machine learning models
ml_method: Grid search cross-validation + AdaBoost for ML optimization (student performance)
sample: 3,518 students
key_finding: Grid search + AdaBoost optimization achieves 84.6-87% accuracy (3,518 students); demonstrates hyperparameter tuning importance for educational prediction; systematic model selection methodology

title: Study-GNN: A Novel Pipeline for Student Performance Prediction Based on Multi-Topology Graph Neural Networks
ml_method: Multi-topology Graph Neural Network (GNN) for student performance prediction
sample: 3983 students across 5 datasets (498, 753, 1179, 1553, 3983)
key_finding: Multi-topology GNN outperforms single-topology baselines (81-82% accuracy); tested across 5 diverse student datasets; captures multi-relational social/academic interactions

title: A Comparative Analysis of On-Device AI-Driven, Self-Regulated Learning and Traditional Pedagogy in University Health Sciences Education
ml_method: On-device LLM (LLama 3.1, Korean Ko-PlatYi-6B) for AI textbooks
sample: 86 students, university health sciences (Korea)
key_finding: On-device AI textbook (LLama 3.1) vs traditional: no significant difference (p>0.05) in exam scores; n=86 health science students; null result highlights current limitations of AI personalization

title: Design optimization of university ideological and political education system based on deep learning
ml_method: CNN-LSTM hybrid for personalized learning path recommendation in Chinese university IPE
sample: Not clearly stated in accessible text
key_finding: CNN-LSTM personalized learning path recommendation for Chinese political education; F1=0.86; limited generalizability due to China-specific IPE context

title: Optimizing learning outcomes: a deep dive into hybrid AI models for adaptive educational feedback
ml_method: Ensemble learning + neural networks on multi-source student data
sample: 32 students (2 real educational datasets)
key_finding: Hybrid ensemble+neural network for student performance prediction (n=32 small); accuracy 67-71%; adaptive feedback generation; small sample limits generalizability

title: Multimodal Detection of Emotional and Cognitive States in E-Learning Through Deep Fusion of Visual and Textual Data with NLP
ml_method: Multimodal fusion: visual (webcam/facial) + textual (NLP) for emotion/cognition detection
sample: 30 training videos + 10 test videos (very small)
key_finding: Multimodal (visual+text) fusion achieves 88.5-91.5% accuracy for emotional/cognitive state detection in e-learning; 30+10 videos (very small pilot); outperforms unimodal approaches

title: Design of performance evaluation method for higher education reform based on adaptive fuzzy algorithm
ml_method: ANFIS (Adaptive Neural Fuzzy Inference System) for teacher performance evaluation
sample: University teachers (exact n not clearly stated)
key_finding: ANFIS achieves 76.4% accuracy for teacher performance evaluation; improves objectivity of higher education assessment; tangential to student-focused RQs

title: Generative AI in the Classroom: Effects of Context-Personalized Learning Material and Tasks on Motivation and Performance
journal: International Journal of Artificial Intelligence in Education
ml_method: GenAI (LLM) for context-personalized educational content generation
sample: 121 primary school students; 2×2 mixed experimental design
key_finding: 2×2 mixed design (n=121): context-personalized GenAI material improves motivation and performance vs standard; first controlled experiment on GenAI educational content personalization in primary education.

title: Towards an AI tutor for undergraduate geotechnical engineering: a comparative study of evaluating the efficiency of large language model application programming interfaces
ml_method: GPT-4 vs Llama-3 (open-source) as AI tutors via API
sample: Geotechnical Engineering exam questions dataset; no student sample
key_finding: Both GPT-4 and Llama-3 effectively generate accurate answers for geotechnical engineering; open-source Llama-3 competitive; demonstrates domain-specific LLM tutoring feasibility.

title: Personalized learning path planning for higher education based on deep generative models and quantum machine learning
ml_method: Transformer + Quantum State Classification + Adversarial Training for learning path planning
sample: 50,000 student learning records (multimodal: text, audio, video)
key_finding: Multimodal Transformer + quantum ML for personalized learning paths (50K student records); adversarial training for robustness; addresses dynamic interest changes; novel quantum-classical hybrid approach.

title: CourseKG: An Educational Knowledge Graph Based on Course Information for Precision Teaching
ml_method: BERT + Knowledge Graph + BiGRU + CRF + MHSA
sample: 100 students, C programming course, 2 Chinese platforms
key_finding: BERT+KG framework for precision teaching; knowledge graph improves course content linking; 100-student evaluation shows improved entity recognition but lacks student learning outcome metrics

title: Gaze-Driven Adaptive Learning System With ChatGPT-Generated Summaries
ml_method: Transformer (InceptionTime) on gaze data + ChatGPT adaptive summaries
sample: N=22 university students (small)
key_finding: Gaze+Transformer predicts engagement (68.15%); ChatGPT summaries as adaptive intervention significantly improve comprehension; N=22 is critical limitation

title: Federated Learning for Data Analytics in Education
ml_method: Federated Learning (horizontal/vertical) for privacy-preserving educational analytics
sample: 77,083 students; 500-1000 students in federated experiments
key_finding: Federated learning preserves student data privacy while maintaining performance comparable to centralized models; 77K students; addresses legal/ethical issues in educational ML

title: Performance of ChatGPT on UK Standardized Admission Tests: Insights From the BMAT, TMUA, LNAT, and TSA Examinations
ml_method: ChatGPT GPT-3.5 evaluation on standardized tests
sample: 509 questions from 4 UK standardized admission tests (BMAT, TMUA, LNAT, TSA), 2019–2022
key_finding: ChatGPT better on aptitude/critical thinking (BMAT sec1 73% ranking) than scientific/math (BMAT sec2 1% ranking); promising supplementary tool for critical thinking and reading comprehension; 509 questions, statistically validated.

title: Sim-GAIL: A generative adversarial imitation learning approach of student modelling for intelligent tutoring systems
ml_method: GAIL (Generative Adversarial Imitation Learning) for student simulation in ITS
sample: 784,309 simulated student interactions; 1,000–2,500 real validation students
key_finding: First GAIL-based student simulation for ITS training; 784K simulated interactions; enables ITS training without large real datasets; novel simulation-to-real transfer for student modelling.

title: Evaluation of the Performance of Generative AI Large Language Models ChatGPT, Google Bard, and Microsoft Bing Chat in Supporting Evidence-Based Dentistry
ml_method: ChatGPT (GPT-3.5/4), Google Bard, Microsoft Bing Chat — comparative mixed methods
sample: 20 clinical questions from dentistry (blind review by experts)
key_finding: LLMs (ChatGPT, Bard, Bing) show potential for evidence-based dentistry but limitations can lead to harmful decisions; critical thinking of dentist cannot be replaced; n=20 questions, mixed-methods.

title: Real-time prediction of science student learning outcomes using machine learning classification of hemodynamics during virtual reality and online learning sessions
ml_method: ML classification of fNIRS hemodynamic data for real-time engagement/attention
sample: 10-40 students/participants (small pilot study)
key_finding: fNIRS hemodynamic data + ML classification for real-time learning outcome prediction; VR + online contexts; n=10-40 (pilot); novel neurocognitive sensing approach for adaptive platforms

title: Automated Data-Driven Generation of Personalized Pedagogical Interventions in Intelligent Tutoring Systems
journal: International Journal of Artificial Intelligence in Education
ml_method: BERT + Reinforcement Learning + Logistic Regression for ITS feedback generation
sample: 20,000 students (primary dataset); 183 students (evaluation set)
key_finding: BERT+RL for automated personalized hint/explanation generation in ITS (20,000 students); Wikipedia-based explanations; outperforms baseline; open-source; strong reproducibility.

title: Associating Facial Expressions and Upper-Body Gestures with Learning Tasks for Enhancing Intelligent Tutoring Systems
journal: International Journal of Artificial Intelligence in Education
ml_method: Computer vision: facial expression recognition + hand gesture detection for ITS
sample: 9 undergraduate CS students (convenience sample, all male)
key_finding: Computer vision for facial expression + gesture detection in ITS; 86.87% accuracy; hand-over-face gestures increase with difficulty; proof-of-concept with small n=9.

title: Interaction, Artificial Intelligence, and Motivation in Children’s Speech Learning and Rehabilitatio
key_finding: The integration of digital serious games into speech learning (rehabilitation) has demonstrated significant potential in enhancing accessibility and inclusivity for children with speech disabilities.

title: Large Language Model-Based Chatbots in Higher Education
key_finding: Large language models (LLMs) are artificial intelligence (AI) platforms capable of analyzing and mimicking natural language processing. Leveraging deep learning, LLM capabilities have been advanced si

key_finding: The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into education is transforming learning across various domains, including dentistry. Endodontic education can significantly benefit from AI chatbots; ho

key_finding: ChatGPT has created considerable anxiety among teachers concerned that students might turn to large language models (LLMs) to write their assignments. Many of these models are able to create grammatic

title: Generative AI in graduate bioprocess engineering exams: Is attention all students need?
key_finding: State-of-the-art large language models (LLMs) can now answer conceptual textbook questions with near-perfect accuracy and perform complex equation derivations, raising significant concerns for higher

title: ChatGPT-supported collaborative argumentation: Integrating collaboration script and argument mapping
key_finding: Argumentation is a complex skill essential for English as a Foreign Language (EFL) students to effectively use their English language and reasoning abilities in writing and speaking. Constructing argu

title: Benchmarking of Large Language Models for the Dental Admission Test
key_finding: Background: Large language models (LLMs) have shown promise in educational applications, but their performance on high-stakes admissions tests, such as the Dental Admission Test (DAT), remains unclear

title: Predictive Analytics in Mobile Education: Evaluating Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and Gradien
key_finding: This study aimed to compare the effectiveness of three predictive algorithms—logistic regression, random forest, and GBM—in predicting course completion using user engagement data from online learning

title: Evaluating the evidence-based potential of six large language models in paediatric dentistry: a comp
key_finding: Purpose: The use of large language models (LLMs) in generative artificial intelligence (AI) is rapidly increasing in dentistry. However, their reliability is yet to be fully founded. This study aims t

key_finding: This study aims to investigate the effects of integrating Chat-GPT usage in language learning model on students' argumentation ability, complex comprehension ability, and critical thinking ability. Th

key_finding: The rapid improvement of artificial intelligence (AI) in the educational domain has opened new possibilities for enhancing the learning experiences for students. This research discusses the critical n

title: Exploring prospects, hurdles, and road ahead for generative artificial intelligence in orthopedic ed
key_finding: Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI), characterized by its ability to generate diverse forms of content including text, images, video and audio, has revolutionized many fields, including medical ed

title: A systematic review of large language models and their implications in medical education
key_finding: Introduction: In the past year, the use of large language models (LLMs) has generated significant interest and excitement because of their potential to revolutionise various fields, including medical

title: The Effect of Flipped Interactive Learning (FIL) Based on ChatGPT on Students’ Skills in a Large Pro
key_finding: Flipped Learning (FL) inverts traditional teaching by delivering content online outside the classroom, allowing class time for interactive, hands-on activities. This approach enhances student engageme

key_finding: Large language models (LLMs) have emerged as a powerful tool for biomedical researchers, demonstrating remarkable capabilities in understanding and generating human-like text. ChatGPT with its Code In

key_finding: One of the most significant research areas in education and Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the earlier prediction of students’ academic achievement. Limited studies have been conducted using Deep Lea

key_finding: The advent of artificial intelligence (AI), particularly large language models (LLMs) like ChatGPT and Gemini, has significantly impacted the educational landscape, offering unique opportunities for l

title: Early prediction of student knowledge in game-based learning with distributed representations of ass
key_finding: Game-based learning environments hold significant promise for facilitating learning experiences that are both effective and engaging. To support individualised learning and support proactive scaffoldi

title: Enhancing academic outcomes through an adaptive learning framework utilizing a novel machine learnin
key_finding: Introduction: E landscapes have been transformed by technological advancements, enabling adaptive and flexible learning through AI-based and decision-oriented adaptive learning systems. The increasing

title: An overview and comparison of supervised data mining techniques for student exam performance predict
key_finding: Recent increase in the availability of learning data has given educational data mining an importance and momentum, in order to better understand and optimize the learning process and environments in w

title: Enhancing professional abilities of university students through digital educational interventions: a study in Kazakhstani universities
ml_method: Pre/post-test design; digital educational interventions (MOOC, online platforms)
sample: 520 students, Toraighyrov University, Pavlodar, Kazakhstan; paired t-tests
key_finding: Digital educational interventions significantly improved professional abilities, creativity, motivation in 520 Kazakhstani university students; pre/post paired t-test; applicable to AI-enhanced learning platform design in Central Asia.

title: Lecturers perceptions of using Artificial Intelligence in Tertiary Education in Uzbekistan
ml_method: Qualitative study; purposive sampling; thematic analysis
sample: 15 lecturers (13 active AI users), higher education in Uzbekistan
key_finding: Most Uzbekistani lecturers (13/15) actively use AI; concerns about student critical thinking and overreliance; first qualitative insight into AI adoption in Central Asian tertiary education.

title: Research trends on artificial intelligence in K-12 education in Asia: a bibliometric analysis using the Scopus database (1996–2025)
ml_method: Bibliometric analysis of 531 Scopus articles (1996–2025)
sample: 531 articles from Scopus database; covers Kazakhstan and Asian countries
key_finding: Kazakhstan grouped in research cluster with Turkey and Iraq; Central Asian AI education research is nascent; identifies gap in K-12 AI research from the region; context for RQ5.

title: Applying game-based learning to a primary school class in computer science terminology learning
ml_method: Game-based learning (GBL platform, no ML algorithm)
sample: n=50 primary school students, Kazakhstan, 10-week experiment, two focus groups
key_finding: 90% of students found GBL beneficial for CS terminology; questionnaire (Impression, Usability, UI) + academic performance measurements; baseline comparison group used
snowball_source: Planning document KZ-28; Frontiers OA; Kazakhstan first-country study

title: Intelligent educational technologies in individual learning: a systematic literature review
ml_method: SLR/Review (PRISMA+AMSTAR2, 933→38 papers)
sample: 38 review articles, 2014-2024, WoS+Scopus+ERIC
key_finding: Adaptive educational environments effective but barriers: technical complexity, teacher upskilling. Kazakhstani lead author (KazNU). Competitor review — no gamification or ML algorithm comparison, general IETs focus.
snowball_source: KZ-29 competitor SLR; cite in Related Work as prior review to differentiate from

title: Artificial Intelligence in Education: AIEd for Personalised Learning Pathways
ml_method: Conceptual review (chatbots, expert systems, ML, personalized systems)
sample: Conceptual paper, 518 citations
key_finding: Framework for AIEd: social networking, chatbots, expert systems, ML, personalized pathways. Kazakhstani authors (Tapalova & Zhiyenbayeva). Conceptual not empirical — cite in Introduction as foundational Kazakhstani work.
snowball_source: KZ-30: Background reference — similar scope to our study but purely educational (no gamification). Cite in Intro/RQ5 discussion as Kazakhstani AI-in-education baseline.

title: Gamification and Artificial Intelligence in Education: A Review of Innovative Approaches to Fostering Critical Thinking
ml_method: Bibliometric analysis (VOSviewer, co-occurrence mapping), 101 publications on gamification + AI + critical thinking
sample: 101 academic publications (bibliometric corpus, 2015–2024)
key_finding: Gamification+AI integration improves student motivation and critical thinking; three dominant research themes identified: adaptive gamified systems, AI-driven feedback, critical thinking assessment; Kazakhstan is an emerging contributor to this research area

title: Development of a smart textbook in informatics with interactive learning support and Kazakh language integration based on artificial intelligence technologies
journal: International Journal of Innovative Research and Scientific Studies
ml_method: Ontological knowledge models + NLP thesauri for automated smart textbook generation in Kazakh language
sample: Prototype evaluation, L.N.Gumilyov Eurasian National University students
key_finding: AI-powered ontological approach enables automated creation of interactive smart textbooks in Kazakh language; addresses digital gap in Kazakh-medium instruction; applicable to computer science courses in Kazakhstan schools
institution: L.N.Gumilyov Eurasian National University, Astana, Kazakhstan

title: Designing an Adaptive Educational Platform for UNT Preparation: A Machine Learning-Based Approach
journal: 2025 IEEE 5th International Conference on Smart Information Systems and Technologies (SIST)
ml_method: ML-based adaptive platform: real-time analytics, topic-specific practice, interactive UNT exam preparation
sample: 109 recent UNT takers and first-year Astana IT University students (quantitative survey)
key_finding: Students demand real-time analytics and personalized feedback for Kazakhstan's UNT; ML-adaptive platform prototype shows strong user acceptance; 89% of students preferred adaptive practice over static materials

title: Design of an Adaptive Educational Platform for Special Needs Students Using Artificial Intelligence
journal: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer Science and Engineering (UBMK)
ml_method: AI-based adaptive learning models; PRISMA 2020 systematic review of 2020–2025 inclusive EdTech + survey design
sample: Systematic review of 36 papers (PRISMA-filtered) + survey of educators in Kazakhstan
key_finding: AI-adaptive platforms for inclusive education show positive outcomes for special needs learners; personalized learning pathways reduce learning gaps by 40–60%; Kazakhstan lacks dedicated inclusive AI platforms — clear gap for the field

title: The Utilisation of Machine Learning Algorithm Support Vector Machine (SVM) for Improving the Adaptive Assessment
journal: 2023 4th International Conference on Computation, Automation and Knowledge Management (ICCAKM)
ml_method: Support Vector Machine (SVM) for adaptive science assessment scoring and personalisation
sample: Student assessment data from Kazakhstan science education context
key_finding: SVM-based adaptive assessment significantly improves personalisation of science evaluations; machine learning outperforms traditional static testing in distinguishing student knowledge levels; practical implementation in Kazakhstani science education
institution: Kazakhstan (Ainur Smagulova, Serik Zharmukhanbetov — Kazakh institutions)

title: Enhancing Predictive Analytics in Education through Collaborative Filtering Algorithms in Knowledge Management
journal: 2023 4th International Conference on Computation, Automation and Knowledge Management (ICCAKM)
ml_method: Collaborative filtering + AI integration for predictive analytics and student knowledge management
sample: High school students in Kazakhstan (evaluation and problem-solving skills assessment)
key_finding: AI-integrated collaborative filtering improves problem-solving and evaluation skills in Kazakhstani high school students; knowledge management systems with ML recommendations outperform traditional instruction in skill development
institution: Kazakhstan (Mukhabat Tastanova, Rafiya Jilkibayeva, Zhanar Arkhabayeva)

title: Using item response theory in machine learning algorithms for student response data
journal: 2021 IEEE International Conference on Smart Information Systems and Technologies (SIST)
ml_method: Item Response Theory (IRT) combined with ML algorithms (clustering + classification) to improve student ability measurement accuracy
sample: Historical student response data from Kazakhstan educational context
key_finding: IRT+ML hybrid approach outperforms standalone IRT in measuring student abilities; ML-enhanced model enables more accurate identification of student knowledge gaps; applicable to adaptive assessment design in Kazakhstan higher education
institution: Kazakhstan (Merembayev T., Amirgaliyeva S., Kozhaly K.)

title: Applying Intelligent Educational Systems in Higher Education: Pedagogical Effectiveness
journal: 2025 66th International Scientific Conference on Information Technology and Management Science of Riga Technical University (ITMS)
ml_method: Empirical evaluation of AI system outcomes: adaptive modules, recommendation systems, automated assessment, chatbots in HE
sample: Empirical data from Kazakhstan higher education institutions (surveys and performance data)
key_finding: AI-powered intelligent educational systems show predominantly positive pedagogical impact in Kazakhstani HE; student achievement improves with adaptive modules and automated assessment; teacher upskilling remains the main barrier to adoption

title: Revolutionizing the Present Education System Through AI-Driven Learning Platform with Hybrid Techniques
journal: 2024 4th International Conference on Advance Computing and Innovative Technologies in Engineering (ICACITE)
ml_method: Review + hybrid AI techniques for personalized and adaptive learning; 21st century skills framework
sample: Review study with implementation analysis from Uzbekistani higher education context
key_finding: AI-driven hybrid learning platforms improve personalisation and adaptive learning in Uzbekistani HE; hybrid AI (combining recommendation, NLP, and assessment algorithms) outperforms single-method approaches; Central Asian HE needs purpose-built AI platforms
institution: Uzbekistan (Muhammadqodir Yunusaliyev, Ibrokhim Yuldashev, Ardasher Yusupov)
